such as: Fake IDs Illegal telecommunications equipment Modification chips - Unlike ransomware which forces the business to either pay up or lose access to the stolen data extortionists threaten to publicly release the collected information. p_and_r_name: [WP] Data pass
why_is_it_bram_if_applicable: A data pass merchant engages is a form of deceptive marketing (e.g., "free trials" leading to hidden subscriptions), this can result in excessive chargebacks, which triggers BRAM enforcement [context: A data pass merchant is a type of merchant that does not directly acquire its customers but instead relies on another entity (such as a lead generator, affiliate, or partner) to pass along customer information to complete transactions. Essentially, the merchant receives customer payment data (e.g., credit card details) from third parties and processes transactions without having direct interaction or a legitimate relationship with the customer. Exsample - buying airline ticket with enrollment to a membership club]

engaging in the sale of weapons, ammunition, and firearms products in a “card not present” environment (such as
ecommerce, mail-order, telephone-order) are not qualified to receive processing services from Wells Fargo Merchant
Services., use case

p_and_r_name: [MC] [VR] Full disclosure of products and services
[VRP] as a best practice is the collection of the following information Mobile device applications

[WPT] Processor’s acceptance of Merchant as user of the Services and the relevant Payment Methods is limited to the use by Merchant of the Services for payment of Merchant’s own products and services and not for any related or unrelated 3rd party.

[VR] For E-commerce Transactions, Merchant is obliged to provide on its website the following information to an Account Holder for every Transaction: complete description of the goods or services offered, returned merchandise and refund policy, customer service contact (including email address and/or telephone number), address, delivery policy, Merchant’s consumer data protection policy and all other legally required information to be provided to the Account Holder in the relevant jurisdiction.

[MC] [VR] Transaction laundering (factoring) – Accepting a transaction that does not result from an act between the cardholder and the merchant or the cardholder and the sponsored merchant. Payment facilitators may deposit a transaction between the cardholder and a sponsored merchant of the payment facilitator but must not deposit a transaction on behalf of another payment facilita
why_is_it_bram_if_applicable: Transaction laundering (aggregation) Link

p_and_r_name: [WP] Social reviews / traffic / reputaion (Monitoring Illegal Transactions AACQ.C17.1) - not used for now

[MC] An Acquirer must verify that the Merchant or ATM owner from which it intends to acquire Transactions is a bona fide business, has sufficient safeguards in place to protect Account data from unauthorized disclosure or use, and complies with applicable laws, and that each POS Transaction will reflect bona fide business between the Merchant or Submerchant and a Cardholder. Procedures for verifying that a Merchant or ATM owner is a bona fide business are set forth in Chapter 7 of the Security Rules and Procedures manual.
